About: It is about a culturally Arabized, Sunni Muslim dynasty of Persian dehqan origin.
Affects: It affects the governance of Khorasan.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Abbasid caliph al-Ma'mun.
It is associated with the Fourth Fitna.
It is associated with the Abbasid caliphate.
It is associated with the Pahlavi script.
It is associated with the Arabic script.
It is associated with the Saffarid dynasty.
It is associated with the Zaydi ruler Hasan ibn Zayd.
It is associated with the "Anarchy at Samarra".
It is associated with the caliph al-Musta'in.
It is associated with the caliph al-Mu'tazz.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Abbasid caliphate.
CreatedAround: It was created around the 9th century.
FoundedBy: It was founded by Tahir ibn Husayn.
HasA: It has a Persian origin.
It has Sunni Muslim faith.
It has military and security commanders.
It has governors of Khorasan.
It has considerable autonomy.
It has viceroys representing Abbasid rule in Persia.
It has tax revenue from Khorasan.
It has a flourishing of agriculture.
It has popularity in the eastern lands of the Abbasid caliphate.
It has expanding influence.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being culturally Arabized.
It is characterized by being Sunni Muslim.
It is characterized by Persian dehqan origin.
It is characterized by ruling as governors of Khorasan.
It is characterized by serving as military and security commanders in Abbasid Baghdad.
It is characterized by loyalty to the Abbasid caliphs.
It is characterized by oppressive policies in Tabaristan.
It is characterized by being highly Arabized in culture.
It is characterized by eagerness to be accepted in the caliphal world.
It is characterized by initiating the process by which Persian became written in the Perso-Arabic script.
HeldIn: It was held in Abbasid Baghdad.
Influenced: It influenced the replacement of the Pahlavi script with the Arabic script in order to write the Persian language.
It influenced the process by which Persian became written in the Perso-Arabic script.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the service to the Abbasids.
It was influenced by the support of al-Ma'mun in the Fourth Fitna.
It was influenced by the civil war between the rival caliphs al-Amin and al-Ma'mun.
IsA: It is a culturally Arabized, Sunni Muslim dynasty.
It is a dynasty of Persian dehqan origin.
It is a partnership between the Abbasids and the Tahirids.
It is a viceroy representing Abbasid rule in Persia.
KnownFor: It is known for ruling as governors of Khorasan.
It is known for serving as military and security commanders in Abbasid Baghdad.
It is known for initiating the process by which Persian became written in the Perso-Arabic script.
LocatedIn: It is located in Abbasid Baghdad.
MemberOf: It is a member of the Abbasid caliphate.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the civil war between the rival caliphs al-Amin and al-Ma'mun.
It occurred during the Fourth Fitna.
It occurred during the "Anarchy at Samarra".
OccurredIn: It occurred in Khorasan.
It occurred in eastern Khorasan.
It occurred in Sistan.
It occurred in Egypt.
It occurred in the Arabian Peninsula.
It occurred in the eastern lands of the Abbasid caliphate.
It occurred in the western parts of the caliphate.
It occurred in Tabaristan.
It occurred in eastern Persia.
It occurred in Baghdad.
OccurredOn: It occurred on 821.
It occurred on 828.
It occurred on 844.
It occurred on 864.
It occurred on 873.
It occurred on 820.
It occurred on 849.
It occurred on 851.
It occurred on 860s.
It occurred on 865.
PartOf: It is part of the Abbasid caliphate.
It is part of the Iranian Intermezzo.
UsedAs: It is used as governors of Khorasan.
It is used as military and security commanders in Abbasid Baghdad.
It is used as viceroys representing Abbasid rule in Persia.
It is used as governors (ashab al-shurta) of Baghdad.
UsedFor: It is used for military and security command.
It is used for governance of Khorasan.
It is used for implementing the Mihna in Baghdad.
It is used for commanding the defense of Baghdad.
UsedIn: It is used in Abbasid Baghdad.
It is used in the caliphal world.
UsedTo: It is used to collect taxes.
It is used to write the Persian language.
It is used to retain the heritage and charm of the Persian language.
It is used to give refuge to the caliph al-Musta'in.
It is used to command the defense of Baghdad.
It is used to force al-Musta'in to abdicate.
It is used to recognize al-Mu'tazz as caliph.
It is used to restore order in Baghdad.
